Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Compelling The Independent Mental Examination Of Plaintiff
Off Calendar Mar-25-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS ANTONIO SANCHEZ, G.A.T. AIRLINE GROUND SUPPORT, INC.'S Motion For An Order Compelling The Independent Mental Examination Of Plaintiff.

No appearances. The tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted. Defendant's motion for an order compelling the independent medical examination of plaintiff is granted. In alleging substantial emotional distress, Plaintiff has put her mental and psychological state at issue, and Defendant therefore has shown good cause to conduct an IME. (Code Civ. Proc. sections 2032.310, 2032.320(a); Vinson v. Superior Court (1987) 43 Cal.3d 833, 839-840.) Although Defendant was required to obtain a prior court order to conduct an IME rather than serving a demand (Code Civ. Proc. section 2032.310(a); Vinson, 43 Cal.3d at 840), Plaintiff did not object to Defendant's demand, and Plaintiff's counsel concedes he offered to provide alternate dates. (See Carpenter v. Superior Court (2006) 141 Cal.App.4th 249, 254-255 [party served demand for IME and responding party agreed that mental evaluation was appropriate, although parties disagreed on specific diagnostic tests and procedures to be used].) Monetary sanctions are not awarded against either party.

Counsel for the plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Not Reported. (302/EPS)
